title: Recursive
content:
As mentioned a couple of entry earlier, my current life is pretty much a forever loop. If I were to write a blog about my daily life, the blog would end with a single entry. But since I don't have anything useful to talk about lately (have I ever?!), I shall talk about my **recursive life** then.

I usually begin the weekdays somewhere around 5:20-5:35am. It depends on how lazy I am on that particular day.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/01.jpg)

About 30 minutes from bed to bath to ready to leave home.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/02.jpg)

I get to work early because I want to avoid the morning traffic. I never really time my driving but I usually arrived at the office around the same time (+/- 10 minutes).

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/03.jpg)

And I am usually the first one at the building. [FinalPiece](http://finalpiece.net) shares the building with another company. We hope to move into our own building someday. But for now, we need more workers. --"

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/04.jpg)

Depending on how hungry I am that morning, my breakfast ranges from (only) a cup of coffee to something along the line of BBQ pork over rice. I had S&P cup cakes that morning.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/06.jpg)

The moment I open Mail.app, my screen turns orange (my color setting for junk mail).

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/07.jpg)

After morning surf on the web and listening to the morning news on TV, it's time to start working. I've been doing a lot of coding lately since we still can't find a full time programmer yet. I'm not whining though, this is what I do best.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/08.jpg)

Time flies. Lunch time has arrived. I usually go out for lunch around 11:30-11:45am or way after 1pm because every places are so crowded at noon.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/09.jpg)

Coffee stand near the office is quite good, better than many coffee shops I've been to. I enjoy the coffee with my daily **feeds**.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/10.jpg)

Back to work again, I believe I was working with timezone conversion on that day. (Which I complaint a lot about how PHP can handle timezone conversion very easily but .NET can't.)

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/13.jpg)

Oh, have I mentioned that my office has flower. Well, it's a fake one but it does add color to the whiteness.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/15.jpg)

I spend most of the time in front of the computer screens. Switching from Macintosh to Windows to Unix and back to Macintosh again.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/16.jpg)

Depending on the amount of works, I don't usually leave the office at the same time (unlike the arrival). But most of the time, I'd stay until 7-8pm. I left a bit early that day.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/17.jpg)

After that it's free time, but I don't usually get right back home. The reason is the same as in the morning, traffic. So by the time I get back home, it's late into the night.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/18.jpg)

I usually head to bed around 11:30pm-1am. And that where the loop goes back to the beginning.

Oh by the way, my recent activity during the day is posting updates to [Twitter](http://twitter.com/macsf). It's kind of fun posting and seeing what other people are doing.

![](https://macsfvent.sgp1.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/media/2007/03/loop/19.jpg)

Luckily, tomorrow (today, actually) is Saturday. I can enjoy another loop of my life -- the sleeping loop.

Good Day Y'all :)
